architecture_plan.title: AlphaEvolve: A Coding Agent for Scientific and Algorithmic Discovery - Demo Implementation
architecture_plan.overview: A modular implementation architecture for demonstrating the core concepts of AlphaEvolve, a coding agent that discovers scientific and algorithmic solutions. The system includes components for task specification, prompt sampling, creative generation, evaluation, evolution, and distributed pipeline execution. It leverages JAX for high-performance computing and uses ResNet architectures with enhanced regularization techniques.

modules.purpose: Defines the problem space and constraints for scientific discovery tasks including mathematical problems, algorithmic challenges, and optimization problems
Generates diverse prompts from natural language descriptions to guide the coding agent's creative generation process
Implements the core code generation mechanism that produces novel algorithms, mathematical proofs, and computational solutions
Assesses generated solutions using metrics such as correctness, efficiency, and novelty to determine fitness for evolution
Applies evolutionary algorithms to iteratively improve code quality through selection, crossover, and mutation operations
Manages distributed execution of experiments across multiple computing nodes for large-scale scientific discovery
Implements ResNet-based neural network models with configurable depth and regularization techniques including weight decay and AdamW optimizer
Automates systematic exploration of hyperparameter spaces to optimize model performance

framework_suggestions: JAX for high-performance numerical computing and automatic differentiation
Haiku (JAX's neural network library) for building neural architectures
Optax for optimizer implementations including AdamW
Flax for additional neural network utilities
Ray or JAXline for distributed computing pipeline management
